name: plex
description: Media server for streaming your movies and TV library with Plex

fields.name: PLEX_PORT
fields.label: App Port
fields.help:
Host port used to open Plex in your browser when not using host networking.
With host mode, Plex stays on 32400.

fields.name: PLEX_CONFIG_LOCATION
fields.label: Plex Config Folder
fields.help: Stores Plex configuration, metadata, transcode settings, and application state.

fields.name: TV_LOCATION
fields.label: TV Library Folder
fields.help: Folder containing your TV library.

fields.name: MOVIES_LOCATION
fields.label: Movies Library Folder
fields.help: Folder containing your movies library.

fields.name: PUID
fields.label: PUID
fields.help: User ID used by the container for file permissions.

fields.name: PGID
fields.label: PGID
fields.help: Group ID used by the container for file permissions.

fields.name: TZ
fields.label: Timezone
fields.help:
Timezone used by Plex logs and scheduled tasks.
Example: Asia/Dubai.

fields.name: PLEX_CLAIM
fields.label: Plex Claim Token
fields.help:
Optional first-run claim token from plex.tv/claim.
Claim tokens expire quickly, usually within about 4 minutes.

fields.name: RESTART_POLICY
fields.label: Restart Policy
fields.help: Controls whether the container restarts automatically.

fields.name: PLEX_NETWORK_MODE
fields.label: Network
fields.help: Choose Host Mode for the LinuxServer recommended Plex setup, Create New Bridge for the default Compose network, or an existing named Docker network.

fields.name: PLEX_IMAGE
fields.label: Image
fields.help:
Docker image used for Plex.
Change this only if you want a different tag or image source.

fields.name: PLEX_VERSION
fields.label: Plex Version Mode
fields.help:
Plex update channel or exact version.
Examples: docker, latest, public, or a specific Plex version string.

fields.name: PLEX_TRANSCODE_MOUNT
fields.label: Transcode Mount
fields.help:
Optional transcode bind mount in the format /host/path:/transcode or /host/path:/transcode:rw.
Useful for read-heavy transcoding workloads or custom temp storage.

fields.name: PLEX_PORT_MAPPING
fields.label: Primary Bridge Port Mapping
fields.help: Primary port mapping used when Plex is not running in host mode.

fields.name: PLEX_BRIDGE_PORT_1900
fields.label: Bridge Port 1900 UDP
fields.help: Optional SSDP discovery port for bridge mode.

fields.name: PLEX_BRIDGE_PORT_5353
fields.label: Bridge Port 5353 UDP
fields.help: Optional mDNS discovery port for bridge mode.

fields.name: PLEX_BRIDGE_PORT_8324
fields.label: Bridge Port 8324
fields.help: Optional companion control port for bridge mode.

fields.name: PLEX_BRIDGE_PORT_32410
fields.label: Bridge Port 32410 UDP
fields.help: Optional GDM discovery port for bridge mode.

fields.name: PLEX_BRIDGE_PORT_32412
fields.label: Bridge Port 32412 UDP
fields.help: Optional GDM discovery port for bridge mode.

fields.name: PLEX_BRIDGE_PORT_32413
fields.label: Bridge Port 32413 UDP
fields.help: Optional GDM discovery port for bridge mode.

fields.name: PLEX_BRIDGE_PORT_32414
fields.label: Bridge Port 32414 UDP
fields.help: Optional GDM discovery port for bridge mode.

fields.name: PLEX_BRIDGE_PORT_32469
fields.label: Bridge Port 32469
fields.help: Optional DLNA port for bridge mode.

fields.name: PLEX_DRI_DEVICE
fields.label: Intel/AMD Hardware Transcoding
fields.detection_required_message: Run Detect Hardware to check whether /dev/dri exists on the Docker host before enabling Intel or AMD transcoding.
fields.help:
Run Detect Hardware first.
Enable this only when /dev/dri is detected on the Docker host and you want Intel, AMD, or compatible ARM hardware transcoding.

fields.name: PLEX_DVB_DEVICE
fields.label: DVB Devices
fields.detection_required_message: Run Detect Hardware to check whether /dev/dvb exists on the Docker host before enabling tuner devices.
fields.help:
Run Detect Hardware first.
Enable this only when /dev/dvb is detected on the Docker host and Plex needs tuner device access.

fields.name: PLEX_RUNTIME
fields.label: Container Runtime
fields.help:
Optional runtime override.
For Nvidia hardware transcoding, this is typically nvidia when the Nvidia container toolkit is installed on the host.

fields.name: PLEX_NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES
fields.label: Nvidia Visible Devices
fields.help:
Optional Nvidia GPU selector such as all or a specific GPU UUID.
This requires Nvidia container toolkit support on the host and usually Container Runtime = nvidia.

fields.name: PLEX_EXTRA_MOUNT_1
fields.label: Extra Mount 1
fields.help: Optional extra bind mount for Plex in the format /host/path:/container/path or /host/path:/container/path:ro.

fields.name: PLEX_EXTRA_MOUNT_2
fields.label: Extra Mount 2
fields.help: Optional extra bind mount for Plex in the format /host/path:/container/path or /host/path:/container/path:ro.

fields.name: PLEX_EXTRA_MOUNT_3
fields.label: Extra Mount 3
fields.help: Optional extra bind mount for Plex in the format /host/path:/container/path or /host/path:/container/path:ro.

fields.name: PLEX_EXTRA_MOUNT_4
fields.label: Extra Mount 4
fields.help: Optional extra bind mount for Plex in the format /host/path:/container/path or /host/path:/container/path:ro.

fields.name: PLEX_EXTRA_MOUNT_5
fields.label: Extra Mount 5
fields.help: Optional extra bind mount for Plex in the format /host/path:/container/path or /host/path:/container/path:ro.

fields.name: PLEX_CONTAINER_NAME
fields.label: Container Name
fields.help: EasyDocker sets the container name from the project name.

fields.name: PLEX_INTERNAL_PORT
fields.label: Internal App Port
fields.help: Recipe-managed Plex internal port.

fields.name: PLEX_CONFIG_TARGET
fields.label: Config Path Inside Container
fields.help: Recipe-managed internal config path.

fields.name: PLEX_TV_TARGET
fields.label: TV Library Path Inside Container
fields.help: Recipe-managed internal TV library path.

fields.name: PLEX_MOVIES_TARGET
fields.label: Movies Library Path Inside Container
fields.help: Recipe-managed internal movies library path.

ui.overview: Plex lets you stream your movies and TV shows from your own media server.
what_happens: A Plex container will be created
Your config, TV library, and movies library folders will be mounted into the container
Host networking is the default because it is the LinuxServer recommended Plex setup
If you switch away from host mode, bridge-specific Plex ports can be reviewed and adjusted under Advanced
You can optionally add a dedicated transcode mount, extra bind mounts, and supported hardware device mappings
ui.data_location: Relative paths are stored inside this app's project folder under the mounted EasyDocker base config path.
